basics.name: Suyash Nayan
basics.label: Systems Engineer | Protocol Engineer | AI/ML Infrastructure Transition
basics.summary: Systems and protocol engineer with a Rust, Go, Java, and C++ background. Experienced in Ethereum execution-layer infrastructure, EVM internals, low-latency P2P networking, benchmarking, and backend data pipelines. Currently pivoting that systems background toward AI inference, ML infrastructure, model-serving systems, and performance engineering.

work.name: Independent Protocol Engineer and OSS Contributor
work.position: Ethereum Infrastructure, MEV, DeFi Systems, and AI/ML Infra Transition
work.summary: Built Rust-based Ethereum and DeFi systems while starting a deliberate transition into AI/ML infrastructure.
highlights: Built ArbRs, a Rust-based multi-protocol arbitrage engine for local Uniswap V3 and Curve simulation.
Built a Reth-based Ethereum P2P mempool crawler for mainnet peer handshakes, transaction ingestion, PostgreSQL indexing, and WebSocket/TUI observability.
Contributed to Reth and Flashbots Rbuilder across execution logic, txpool/networking paths, metrics, CLI tooling, and builder-session lifecycle improvements.

work.name: The Linux Foundation - Hyperledger Besu
work.summary: Optimized Besu private-network block building and transaction processing.
highlights: Reduced transaction latency by 61% across QBFT, IBFT, and Clique benchmarking environments.
Built isolated benchmarking setups for throughput, latency, CPU, memory, and disk behavior under load.
Presented performance findings at Open Source Summit Japan in Tokyo.

work.name: Google Summer of Code - Chromium, Google
work.summary: Worked in Chromium's C++ Blink rendering engine.
highlights: Designed a testing framework that reduced untested drag-and-drop interaction scenarios by 30%.
Refactored the Automations API for cross-browser drag-and-drop behavior across Chrome, Safari, and Firefox.

projects.name: ArbRs
projects.description: Rust multi-protocol arbitrage engine with local Uniswap V3 and Curve simulation, graph route discovery, and profitability modeling.

projects.name: Reth P2P Mempool Crawler
projects.description: Standalone Ethereum P2P ingestion pipeline using Reth components, Tokio, PostgreSQL, WebSockets, and TUI observability.

projects.name: AI/ML Systems Exercises
projects.description: Practical learning projects around inference, evaluation, observability, and model-serving infrastructure.

projects.name: FlowMark
projects.description: Go-based blockchain load-testing tool and second-place Flow Blockchain Hackathon project.
